notes:
**New**

- **Mention cards got a cleaner, consistent way to act on them.** Hover any
  mention card for quick actions — **pin** it to the top, **open** the source
  note, or **jump to the exact spot** it's mentioned (which scrolls to and briefly
  highlights that line). The references sidebar additionally lets you **dismiss** a
  card. (Mentions Build Spec v0.5.)
- **One way to open and fold mentions.** Click anywhere on a card to expand it;
  ⌘-click opens the note in a new tab, ⇧-click opens it in the sidebar. A new caret
  on the "Mentioned in N notes" header folds the whole section away in one click.
- **Pin a mention once, everywhere.** Pinning a card at the bottom of a note also
  pins it in that note's references sidebar — pinned cards float to the top in both.

**Fixes & polish**

- The mentions header now adapts to its width: in narrow columns (the sidebar,
  split panes, small windows) the sort/fold/filter controls become compact icons
  and the label truncates, instead of overflowing.
- Mention cards show up to three levels of nested context, then a faint "⋯" to
  indicate the branch continues (open the mention to read deeper).
- A mention from a nested block no longer appears twice; the parent's card already
  contains it.

**Under the hood**

- Replaced the two card styles with a single interaction model (the card head is
  the toggle; the title is no longer a separate link), driven per-mount only by its
  action set. Mentions render in pure document flow (always the last block; an empty
  note simply leads with them). Citing the Mentions Build Spec v0.5 (D7–D17).
